steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left side: From 2009 to 2019, travelers flew out of major urban airports with increasing [blank] is an independent clause. Right side: Austin-Bergstrom International Airport in Austin, Texas, saw a 111.3 percent increase in airline passengers... is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be properly separated by a period, semicolon, or comma with conjunction. Option B correctly uses a period, allowing For example, to start a new sentence that introduces an example of the preceding claim.

Options A, C, and D violate this rule: Option A awkwardly ends the first sentence with for example rather than having it introduce the second sentence, Option C misapplies a semicolon before a transitional phrase, and Option D creates a comma splice by using only commas between independent clauses.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
